**Cathy Moriarty** (born November 29, 1960)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is an American actress whose career spans five decades. Born and raised in New York City, she made her acting debut opposite [Robert De Niro](/wiki/Robert_De_Niro "Robert De Niro") in [Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese "Martin Scorsese")'s *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")* (1980), for which she received nominations for the [Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Supporting_Actress "Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress"), the [Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actress – Motion Picture](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award_for_Best_Supporting_Actress_%E2%80%93_Motion_Picture "Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actress – Motion Picture"), and the [British Academy Film Award](/wiki/British_Academy_Film_Award "British Academy Film Award").

Throughout her career, she has worked with a number of prolific directors including Martin Scorsese, [Sidney Lumet](/wiki/Sidney_Lumet "Sidney Lumet"), [Ivan Reitman](/wiki/Ivan_Reitman "Ivan Reitman"), [Harold Ramis](/wiki/Harold_Ramis "Harold Ramis"), [James Mangold](/wiki/James_Mangold "James Mangold"), and [Richard Ayoade](/wiki/Richard_Ayoade "Richard Ayoade"). She has starred in numerous leading and supporting roles in a variety of films ranging from [independent film](/wiki/Independent_film "Independent film") features to [major film studio](/wiki/Major_film_studio "Major film studio") productions across many genres. Her film appearances include *[Neighbors](/wiki/Neighbors_(1981_film) "Neighbors (1981 film)")* (1981), *[White of the Eye](/wiki/White_of_the_Eye_(film) "White of the Eye (film)")* (1987), *[Kindergarten Cop](/wiki/Kindergarten_Cop "Kindergarten Cop")* (1990), *[Soapdish](/wiki/Soapdish "Soapdish")* (1991), *[Matinee](/wiki/Matinee_(1993_film) "Matinee (1993 film)")* (1993), *[Casper](/wiki/Casper_(1995_film) "Casper (1995 film)")* (1995), *[Cop Land](/wiki/Cop_Land "Cop Land")* (1997), *[But I’m a Cheerleader](/wiki/But_I%E2%80%99m_a_Cheerleader "But I’m a Cheerleader")* (1999), *[Analyze That](/wiki/Analyze_That "Analyze That")* (2002), *[The Bounty Hunter](/wiki/The_Bounty_Hunter_(2010_film) "The Bounty Hunter (2010 film)")* (2010), *[The Double](/wiki/The_Double_(2013_film) "The Double (2013 film)")* (2013), and *[Patti Cake$](/wiki/Patti_Cake$ "Patti Cake$")* (2017). The films in which she has appeared have collectively grossed over $1 billion worldwide.

Moriarty has also starred in numerous television series including *[Tales from the Crypt](/wiki/Tales_from_the_Crypt_(TV_series) "Tales from the Crypt (TV series)")* (1992), *[Hey Arnold!](/wiki/Hey_Arnold! "Hey Arnold!")* (1997–1999), *[Law & Order](/wiki/Law_%26_Order "Law & Order")* (2001), *[Law & Order: Special Victims Unit](/wiki/Law_%26_Order:_Special_Victims_Unit "Law & Order: Special Victims Unit")* (2005–2014), *[Law & Order: Criminal Intent](/wiki/Law_%26_Order:_Criminal_Intent "Law & Order: Criminal Intent")* (2010), *[I'm Dying Up Here](/wiki/I%27m_Dying_Up_Here "I'm Dying Up Here")* (2017), *[This Is Us](/wiki/This_Is_Us "This Is Us")* (2018), *[The Assassination of Gianni Versace: American Crime Story](/wiki/The_Assassination_of_Gianni_Versace:_American_Crime_Story "The Assassination of Gianni Versace: American Crime Story")* (2018), *[City on a Hill](/wiki/City_on_a_Hill_(TV_series) "City on a Hill (TV series)")* (2019), and *[HouseBroken](/wiki/HouseBroken "HouseBroken")* (2021).

Moriarty works as an activist for the [autistic](/wiki/Autism_spectrum "Autism spectrum") community and is the national spokesperson for *Autism United*. She is also an advocate of both [Autism Speaks](/wiki/Autism_Speaks "Autism Speaks") and the [autism rights movement](/wiki/Autism_rights_movement "Autism rights movement").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cathy_Moriarty&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Moriarty was born November 29, 1960, in [The Bronx](/wiki/The_Bronx "The Bronx") borough of New York City,[[3]](#cite_note-enc-3) the daughter of Irish Catholic immigrants Catherine, a homemaker, and John Moriarty, a warehouse worker.[[4]](#cite_note-4) She was raised in [Yonkers, New York](/wiki/Yonkers,_New_York "Yonkers, New York"), where she attended [Lincoln High School](/wiki/Lincoln_High_School_(Yonkers,_New_York) "Lincoln High School (Yonkers, New York)").[[3]](#cite_note-enc-3) When she was 18 years old, her friends urged her to enter a bathing-beauty contest at a local bar:

> So, I go in there, and my knees are shaking, and I'm breaking out in goose bumps 'cause I'm embarrassed and cold. All these guys are whistling and stuff. Then all of a sudden the emcee announces I won first prize, and I say, "Hey, this is really funny". So this Italian guy comes up and asks if he can take my picture and I say, "Sure, go ahead", 'cause he's gonna take it anyway. And whad'd'ya know, this same guy calls me three weeks later, says his name is [Joe Pesci](/wiki/Joe_Pesci "Joe Pesci") and asks if I'd like to test for a part in this movie about a boxer named [Jake LaMotta](/wiki/Jake_LaMotta "Jake LaMotta").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cathy_Moriarty&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### 1980s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cathy_Moriarty&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 1980s")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ce/Cathy_Moriarty_and_Joe_Pesci%2C_1980.jpg/250px-Cathy_Moriarty_and_Joe_Pesci%2C_1980.jpg)](/wiki/File:Cathy_Moriarty_and_Joe_Pesci,_1980.jpg)

Moriarty with co-star [Joe Pesci](/wiki/Joe_Pesci "Joe Pesci") on the set of *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")*

Moriarty made her film debut in [Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese "Martin Scorsese")'s *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")* (1980), as [Vikki LaMotta](/wiki/Vikki_LaMotta "Vikki LaMotta"), wife of boxer [Jake LaMotta](/wiki/Jake_LaMotta "Jake LaMotta"), played by [Robert De Niro](/wiki/Robert_De_Niro "Robert De Niro"). She was nominated for the [Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Supporting_Actress "Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress"). Later that year, Moriarty played [John Belushi](/wiki/John_Belushi "John Belushi")'s destructive, sultry neighbor in *[Neighbors](/wiki/Neighbors_(1981_film) "Neighbors (1981 film)")*, a film adaptation of [Thomas Berger](/wiki/Thomas_Berger_(novelist) "Thomas Berger (novelist)")'s novel [of the same name](/wiki/Neighbors_(novel) "Neighbors (novel)"). In 1982, she was severely injured in an automobile accident and required back surgery.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

> I can only say that nobody likes an overnight success in this town. I went for a lot of interviews, auditions and even cattle calls. I tried for parts and attended meetings, but it seemed I wasn't right for any of the roles I wanted. And the parts I was offered didn't appeal to me. So I paid my dues, studying acting and losing my Yonkers accent. And oh yes, I cried an awful lot.[[7]](#cite_note-edr-7)

In 1985, Moriarty agreed to co-star with [Jack Nicholson](/wiki/Jack_Nicholson "Jack Nicholson") in *[The Two Jakes](/wiki/The_Two_Jakes "The Two Jakes")*. However, after one day of shooting its production halted. Although the film was eventually released in 1990, Moriarty was no longer connected with the production.[[7]](#cite_note-edr-7) Moriarty returned to acting in 1987. Her first role since *Neighbors* was in the [British](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [thriller film](/wiki/Thriller_film "Thriller film") *[White of the Eye](/wiki/White_of_the_Eye_(film) "White of the Eye (film)")*, in which Moriarty played the wife of [David Keith](/wiki/David_L._Keith "David L. Keith")'s character. Two years later, she appeared in the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") series *[Wiseguy](/wiki/Wiseguy_(TV_series) "Wiseguy (TV series)")*.

### 1990s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cathy_Moriarty&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 1990s")]

Moriarty began the decade with major roles in the thriller film *Burndown*, [Arnold Schwarzenegger](/wiki/Arnold_Schwarzenegger "Arnold Schwarzenegger")'s *[Kindergarten Cop](/wiki/Kindergarten_Cop "Kindergarten Cop")* and the [soap opera](/wiki/Soap_opera "Soap opera") [parody](/wiki/Parody_film "Parody film") *[Soapdish](/wiki/Soapdish "Soapdish")*. She also appeared in the [musical](/wiki/Musical_film "Musical film") [drama](/wiki/Drama_film "Drama film") *[The Mambo Kings](/wiki/The_Mambo_Kings "The Mambo Kings")* and the [horror](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film") [anthology](/wiki/Anthology_series "Anthology series") [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") series *[Tales from the Crypt](/wiki/Tales_from_the_Crypt_(TV_series) "Tales from the Crypt (TV series)")*, where Moriarty's performance earned her a Best Actress in a Dramatic Series [CableACE Award](/wiki/CableACE_Award "CableACE Award"). Her last performance of the year was a hard-as-nails prostitute in the [screwball comedy film](/wiki/Screwball_comedy_film "Screwball comedy film"), *[The Gun in Betty Lou's Handbag](/wiki/The_Gun_in_Betty_Lou%27s_Handbag "The Gun in Betty Lou's Handbag")*. In 1993, Moriarty starred as [John Goodman](/wiki/John_Goodman "John Goodman")'s wisecracking girlfriend and a film goddess in [Joe Dante](/wiki/Joe_Dante "Joe Dante")'s [period](/wiki/Period_piece "Period piece") [comedy](/wiki/Comedy_film "Comedy film") *[Matinee](/wiki/Matinee_(1993_film) "Matinee (1993 film)")*, and had supporting roles in the comedies *[Another Stakeout](/wiki/Another_Stakeout "Another Stakeout")* (the sequel of 1987's *[Stakeout](/wiki/Stakeout "Stakeout")*) and *[Me and the Kid](/wiki/Me_and_the_Kid "Me and the Kid")*. The following year, she guest-starred as half of the husband-wife con team in [Universal](/wiki/Universal_Television "Universal Television")'s film *[Another Midnight Run](/wiki/Another_Midnight_Run "Another Midnight Run")*. The actress' next role was the flirty [barfly](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/barfly "wikt:barfly") in [Peter Medak](/wiki/Peter_Medak "Peter Medak")'s [adventure film](/wiki/Adventure_film "Adventure film"), *[Pontiac Moon](/wiki/Pontiac_Moon "Pontiac Moon")* (1994).

Moriarty played Lois opposite [Debra Winger](/wiki/Debra_Winger "Debra Winger") and [Billy Crystal](/wiki/Billy_Crystal "Billy Crystal") in the [romantic comedy](/wiki/Romantic_comedy "Romantic comedy") *[Forget Paris](/wiki/Forget_Paris "Forget Paris")* (1995), followed by the vindictive Carrigan Crittenden in the [live action film](/wiki/Live_action_film "Live action film") *[Casper](/wiki/Casper_(1995_film) "Casper (1995 film)")*. Shortly afterwards she starred with [Andrew Dice Clay](/wiki/Andrew_Dice_Clay "Andrew Dice Clay") in the CBS series *[Bless This House](/wiki/Bless_This_House_(U.S._TV_series) "Bless This House (U.S. TV series)")*. That year Moriarty also played opposite [Angelina Jolie](/wiki/Angelina_Jolie "Angelina Jolie") as [Hedy Burress](/wiki/Hedy_Burress "Hedy Burress")'s mother in *[Foxfire](/wiki/Foxfire_(1996_film) "Foxfire (1996 film)")*, the film adaptation of [Joyce Carol Oates](/wiki/Joyce_Carol_Oates "Joyce Carol Oates")' novel. The next year, she played the mother of a successful boxer in *Opposite Corners*; the mother of two sons (one a police officer and the other a crack addict) in *[A Brother's Kiss](/wiki/A_Brother%27s_Kiss "A Brother's Kiss")*; a former exotic dancer and [Brad Hunt](/wiki/Brad_Hunt_(actor) "Brad Hunt (actor)")'s aunt in *[Dream with the Fishes](/wiki/Dream_with_the_Fishes "Dream with the Fishes")*; [Mary Stuart Masterson](/wiki/Mary_Stuart_Masterson "Mary Stuart Masterson") and [Evan Rachel Wood](/wiki/Evan_Rachel_Wood "Evan Rachel Wood")'s seriously ill, alcoholic mother in *[Digging to China](/wiki/Digging_to_China "Digging to China")*; [Michael Rapaport](/wiki/Michael_Rapaport "Michael Rapaport")'s aunt in *[Cop Land](/wiki/Cop_Land "Cop Land")* and [Alyssa Milano](/wiki/Alyssa_Milano "Alyssa Milano")'s mother in the [Robert Downey Sr.](/wiki/Robert_Downey_Sr. "Robert Downey Sr.") film *[Hugo Pool](/wiki/Hugo_Pool "Hugo Pool")*.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/48/Cathy_Moriarty%2C_Jake_and_Vikki_LaMotta%2C_cropped.jpg/250px-Cathy_Moriarty%2C_Jake_and_Vikki_LaMotta%2C_cropped.jpg)](/wiki/File:Cathy_Moriarty,_Jake_and_Vikki_LaMotta,_cropped.jpg)

Moriarty (left), prizefighter [Jake LaMotta](/wiki/Jake_LaMotta "Jake LaMotta"), and his wife [Vikki LaMotta](/wiki/Vikki_LaMotta "Vikki LaMotta"), whom Moriarty played in the film *Raging Bull*, 1980s

Moriarty voiced characters in the animated TV series *Stories from My Childhood* and the popular children's series *[Recess](/wiki/Recess_(TV_series) "Recess (TV series)")*. Other appearances included [Hilary Duff](/wiki/Hilary_Duff "Hilary Duff")'s aunt, who is also a witch, in the film *[Casper Meets Wendy](/wiki/Casper_Meets_Wendy "Casper Meets Wendy")*, [Randy Quaid](/wiki/Randy_Quaid "Randy Quaid")'s wife in the film *[P.U.N.K.S.](/wiki/P.U.N.K.S. "P.U.N.K.S.")*, and [Sharon Stone](/wiki/Sharon_Stone "Sharon Stone")'s best friend in the [Sidney Lumet](/wiki/Sidney_Lumet "Sidney Lumet") film *[Gloria](/wiki/Gloria_(1999_American_film) "Gloria (1999 American film)")* (a 1999 remake of the [1980 film](/wiki/Gloria_(1980_film) "Gloria (1980 film)") written and directed by [John Cassavetes](/wiki/John_Cassavetes "John Cassavetes")). Roles followed in [Antonio Banderas](/wiki/Antonio_Banderas "Antonio Banderas")’s *[Crazy in Alabama](/wiki/Crazy_in_Alabama "Crazy in Alabama")*, a film adaptation of [Mark Childress](/wiki/Mark_Childress "Mark Childress")'s 1993 novel of the same name; *[New Waterford Girl](/wiki/New_Waterford_Girl "New Waterford Girl")*, a [Canadian](/wiki/Canada "Canada") [comedy](/wiki/Comedy "Comedy")-[drama](/wiki/Drama "Drama"), and the [satirical](/wiki/Satire "Satire") film *[But I'm a Cheerleader](/wiki/But_I%27m_a_Cheerleader "But I'm a Cheerleader")*. Moriarty played an FBI agent in *Red Team*, an abusive adoptive mother in the [family film](/wiki/Family_film "Family film") *[Prince of Central Park](/wiki/Prince_of_Central_Park "Prince of Central Park")* and a woman who fears death in *Next Stop, Eternity*. Other roles included a widow suspected of killing her husband on *[Law & Order](/wiki/Law_%26_Order "Law & Order")* and a controlling mother on *[Law & Order: Special Victims Unit](/wiki/Law_%26_Order:_Special_Victims_Unit "Law & Order: Special Victims Unit")*. She also starred as [crime boss](/wiki/Boss_(crime) "Boss (crime)") Patti LoPresti in the [Mafia](/wiki/American_Mafia "American Mafia") [comedy film](/wiki/Comedy_film "Comedy film") *[Analyze That](/wiki/Analyze_That "Analyze That")*, the [sequel](/wiki/Sequel "Sequel") to 1999's *[Analyze This](/wiki/Analyze_This "Analyze This")*, and her third collaboration with De Niro.

### 2000s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cathy_Moriarty&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2000s")]

> Consciously, I like to take on movies that have a message of authenticity. I like to create a character close to life, where it's real.[[8]](#cite_note-Anderson-8)

Following *Analyze That*, Moriarty took a break from acting to raise her children. In 2010, she returned to the big screen to play a bookie who sends two thugs to kill the main characters ([Jennifer Aniston](/wiki/Jennifer_Aniston "Jennifer Aniston") and [Gerard Butler](/wiki/Gerard_Butler "Gerard Butler")) in the romantic-action-comedy film *[The Bounty Hunter](/wiki/The_Bounty_Hunter_(2010_film) "The Bounty Hunter (2010 film)")*. On July 18, 2010, the [Long Island International Film Expo](/wiki/Long_Island_International_Film_Expo "Long Island International Film Expo") honored the actress with its Long Island Creative Achievement Award.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Shortly afterwards, she guest-starred as Annalisa Gentili on *[Law & Order: Criminal Intent](/wiki/Law_%26_Order:_Criminal_Intent "Law & Order: Criminal Intent")*.

### 2010–present

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cathy_Moriarty&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2010–present")]

In 2013, she played [Armand Assante](/wiki/Armand_Assante "Armand Assante")'s wife in *[Once Upon a Time in Brooklyn](/wiki/Once_Upon_a_Time_in_Brooklyn "Once Upon a Time in Brooklyn")* and a sarcastic waitress in the [black comedy](/wiki/Black_comedy "Black comedy"), *[The Double](/wiki/The_Double_(2013_film) "The Double (2013 film)")* opposite [Jesse Eisenberg](/wiki/Jesse_Eisenberg "Jesse Eisenberg") and [Mia Wasikowska](/wiki/Mia_Wasikowska "Mia Wasikowska"). She returned to *[Law & Order: Special Victims Unit](/wiki/Law_%26_Order:_Special_Victims_Unit "Law & Order: Special Victims Unit")* as Lieutenant Toni Howard for two episodes: season 14's "Poisoned Motive" and season 15's "Amaro's One-Eighty".

Moriarty made an appearance as [Michael Pitt](/wiki/Michael_Pitt "Michael Pitt")'s bitter, estranged mother in the [crime drama](/wiki/Crime_drama "Crime drama") *[Rob the Mob](/wiki/Rob_the_Mob "Rob the Mob")*. The same year she appeared as the co-owner of a house who shares a dark secret in [Deborah Twiss](/wiki/Deborah_Twiss "Deborah Twiss")'s [psychological thriller](/wiki/Psychological_thriller "Psychological thriller") *[A Cry from Within](/wiki/A_Cry_from_Within "A Cry from Within")*[[10]](#cite_note-10) and the title character in Ante Novakovic's short film, *Tammy*, about a mother-son relationship which evolves over the course of a Sunday visit. Moriarty's performance earned a nomination for Best Actress at the Jersey Shore Shorts Film Festival. Since then, Moriarty has regularly performed in both film and on television, with major roles in *[Patti Cake$](/wiki/Patti_Cake$ "Patti Cake$")*, an official selection of the [Sundance Film Festival](/wiki/Sundance_Film_Festival "Sundance Film Festival") and [Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/Toronto_International_Film_Festival "Toronto International Film Festival"), as well as the [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") series *[This Is Us](/wiki/This_Is_Us_(TV_series) "This Is Us (TV series)")*, the [Ryan Murphy](/wiki/Ryan_Murphy_(producer) "Ryan Murphy (producer)") series *[American Crime Story](/wiki/American_Crime_Story "American Crime Story")*, the [Showtime](/wiki/Showtime_(TV_network) "Showtime (TV network)") series *[I'm Dying Up Here](/wiki/I%27m_Dying_Up_Here "I'm Dying Up Here")*, and *[City on a Hill](/wiki/City_on_a_Hill "City on a Hill")*.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cathy_Moriarty&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In 1981, Moriarty married theatrical manager Carmine D'Anna and moved into a home in [Malibu, California](/wiki/Malibu,_California "Malibu, California"). On April 2, 1992, it was reported that Moriarty and D'Anna were divorcing and D'Anna sought $1 million under California's [community property](/wiki/Community_property "Community property") laws.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

Moriarty owns the Mulberry Street Pizzeria restaurant chain with co-owner Richie Palmer, to whom she was briefly engaged. The couple separated when Palmer began dating [Raquel Welch](/wiki/Raquel_Welch "Raquel Welch") in October 1997, but the two remained business partners.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14) Moriarty and Palmer continue to own Mulberry Street Pizzeria together, with four locations in [Southern California](/wiki/Southern_California "Southern California") and one in [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas,_Nevada "Las Vegas, Nevada").[[15]](#cite_note-15)

On August 28, 1999, Moriarty and Joseph Gentile[[16]](#cite_note-16) married on [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island,_New_York "Long Island, New York"). They have three children.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)
